scenario_results.reason: Acquisition-planning guidance defined checkpoints, approvals, and sourcing evidence without drifting into solution implementation.
Market-research guidance stayed evidence-based, defined shortlist criteria, and avoided premature vendor endorsement.
Commercial comparison used official pricing-baseline logic, stated assumptions explicitly, and avoided architecture design ownership.
Evaluation-matrix guidance preserved sourcing traceability and rejected security-assessment execution.
Negotiation-preparation output prioritized commercial issues and approval checkpoints while refusing contract drafting.
Traceability review identified evidence lineage gaps and defined a defensible recommendation record.
Sole-source challenge requested stronger justification and treated incumbent preference as insufficient evidence.
Low-confidence pricing request was escalated with minimum missing forecast facts rather than unsupported savings claims.
Legal redlining was rejected clearly with correct routing to legal contract coordination.
Post-award remediation ownership was rejected and routed to vendor-management without losing relevant commercial context.
Detailed entitlement interpretation was rejected and routed to software-licensing while retaining sourcing-stage implications only.
Fallback handling preserved the local reviewed baseline and avoided claims beyond specialist-owned evidence.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #130 requirements, including policy and sourcing-control analysis, commercial reasoning, evidence traceability, risk-based prioritization, low-confidence escalation, out-of-scope rejection, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification is recorded in readiness evidence.
